1.journal: Phase 5 gate. Strong start. Focused on CI context.

16.focusNote: Build complete. Interview prep is live risk.

problems.title: Two Sum + Contains Duplicate
problems.pattern: HashMap for value→index lookup; HashSet for seen elements

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock + Binary Search
Top K Frequent Elements
problems.pattern: Min-heap size k or bucket sort

fileDefense.title: DemoToolbar.tsx
fileDefense.why: Operator controls: case selector, load, start-over. Not product core — demo tooling that reduces live-demo risk.
fileDefense.terminology: Component composition · operator tooling · fixture injection
fileDefense.interviewLine: Not product core — demo tooling that reduces live-demo risk. The test fixtures and production concerns stay separate.

fileDefense.title: demoCases.ts
fileDefense.why: Reusable typed synthetic demo data; prefilled reviewer findings. Keeps demo data outside components.
fileDefense.terminology: Separation of concerns · fixtures · synthetic-data boundary
fileDefense.interviewLine: Demo data lives outside components so UI logic stays testable and the demo is repeatable without typing.

fileDefense.title: reviewApi.ts
fileDefense.why: Transport boundary to Spring; typed AsyncState returns; error mapping; no React state.
fileDefense.interviewLine: Fetch logic should not scatter across presentational components — this boundary prevents that.

fileDefense.title: types.ts
fileDefense.why: Shared DTO and UI types; AsyncState<T> discriminated union; ApiError.
fileDefense.terminology: Discriminated union · exhaustive switch · impossible states
fileDefense.interviewLine: Types make state explicit at compile time; runtime validation still matters at the network boundary.

fileDefense.title: RagEngineClient (interface)
fileDefense.why: Stable Java abstraction for the Python engine. Hides subprocess details.
fileDefense.interviewLine: Controllers depend on the contract, not subprocess details — swapping to HTTP later doesn't change the controller.

fileDefense.title: PythonProcessRagEngineClient
fileDefense.why: Subprocess launch, JSON stdin/stdout, exit-code handling, timeout, error envelope parsing.
fileDefense.terminology: Adapter pattern · process boundary · error envelope
fileDefense.interviewLine: All Java-to-Python integration risk is isolated here.

fileDefense.title: GlobalExceptionHandler
fileDefense.why: Maps all exceptions to structured ApiErrorResponse with requestId.
fileDefense.terminology: Cross-cutting concern · exception mapping · stable error contract
fileDefense.interviewLine: Clients need stable error shapes, not stack traces.

fileDefense.title: ingestion.py
fileDefense.why: SOP loading, frontmatter parsing, chunking with stable chunk_ids, chunks.jsonl output.
fileDefense.interviewLine: Ingestion is where silent quality loss begins.
